previousChapter.title: Market Analysis
previousChapter.mission: Define the market, buyer, sizing, drivers, and constraints that govern adoption and valuation.
contentRequirements: Market boundary, included/excluded spend, adjacencies, and status-quo substitutes.
TAM/SAM/SOM or evidence-constrained sizing using multiple lenses, not one broad estimate.
Buyer/user/payer segmentation with budget ownership and adoption path.
Growth drivers and adoption constraints (regulation, switching cost, ROI, trust, capital intensity).
Sizing/adoption diligence gaps and contradictory estimates preserved.

plannedTables.name: Market definition table
plannedTables.requirement: segment/category, included spend, excluded spend, buyer/payer, relevance.

plannedTables.name: TAM/SAM/SOM or sizing lens table
plannedTables.requirement: publisher, year, geography, value, CAGR, methodology, confidence, limitation.

plannedTables.name: Segment / buyer map
plannedTables.requirement: segment, buyer, user, payer, workflow, budget owner, adoption trigger.

plannedTables.name: Growth drivers and constraints table
plannedTables.requirement: driver/constraint, direction, timing, implication, diligence ask.

plannedFigures.name: Market sizing lens
plannedFigures.requirement: TAM/SAM/SOM or constrained sizing layers with numeric values when supported.

plannedFigures.name: Market estimate range
plannedFigures.requirement: Low/base/high estimates of one market quantity (e.g. TAM, SAM, penetration, growth forecast) with source-backed bounds and one consistent unit. Do not mix incompatible units (percent vs $B vs index) across rows.

evidenceStrategy: Market data from analyst reports, government/industry statistics, filings, trade associations, public-company disclosures.
Buyer/budget evidence from procurement pages, customer case studies, public budgets, job postings, reimbursement data.
Disconfirming evidence from slowdown reports, failed pilots, regulatory bottlenecks, saturation, contrary estimates.
qualityBar: Define the market boundary before sizing it.
Tie drivers and constraints to adoption timing, budget owner, and valuation relevance.
Do not rely on one generic TAM estimate or call the market large without boundary logic.
If SAM/SOM cannot be isolated, preserve failed sizing paths and diligence asks.
Major chart numbers must match source-backed table numbers or state transformations in approximationNotes.

chapter.title: Competitors
chapter.mission: Identify direct, incumbent, adjacent, substitute, and status-quo alternatives, and assess differentiation durability.
contentRequirements: Landscape covering direct peers, incumbents, adjacents, substitutes, status quo, internal build, and likely entrants.
Competitor profiles with scale, funding, target customer, product scope, pricing, and strategic direction.
Comparisons on capability, pricing, GTM/distribution, and trust/regulatory posture.
Switching cost, lock-in, multi-homing, distribution power, and supply/partner access.
Moat durability, commoditization or displacement risk, and adverse competitor evidence.

plannedTables.name: Competitor profile table
plannedTables.requirement: competitor, category, scale/funding, target segment, differentiation, limitation.

plannedTables.name: Feature / capability matrix
plannedTables.requirement: buying criteria, company, competitors, unsupported cells marked clearly.

plannedTables.name: Pricing / packaging comparison
plannedTables.requirement: price/unit/contract model, included capabilities, discount or unknowns, implication.

plannedTables.name: Moat durability / competitive risk register
plannedTables.requirement: moat claim, threat, severity, mitigation/diligence ask.

plannedFigures.name: Competitive positioning map
plannedFigures.requirement: Numeric axes only when source-backed; otherwise evidence-backed ordinal scoring with rationale.

plannedFigures.name: Feature breadth / capability map
plannedFigures.requirement: Capability coverage and strength by competitor.

evidenceStrategy: Competitor official surfaces — product, pricing, docs, case studies, security/compliance, integrations, channel pages, changelog.
Independent proof — benchmarks, reviews, filings, market reports, customer references, procurement pages, adverse reporting.
Never treat vendor-authored comparison pages as independent proof.
qualityBar: Compare every material way the buyer can solve the same job, including status quo and internal build.
Mark unsupported matrix cells as unknown or evidence gaps; never guess.
Include adverse/disconfirming evidence on displacement, commoditization, channel power, or incumbent response.
Query by competitor class; do not build the matrix from one generic comparison source.

nextChapter.title: Financials
nextChapter.mission: Explain revenue model, unit economics, capital needs, and which financial inputs are insufficient for underwriting.
contentRequirements: Revenue streams, pricing model, revenue mix, and recognition issues where supportable.
GTM motion and sales efficiency proxies (cycle, CAC/payback or proxy, channel economics).
Cost structure, gross margin drivers, working capital, capex, and service-delivery costs.
Public traction (revenue, ARR, GMV, units, locations, utilization, active users) versus private-metric gaps.

Capital adequacy and financing dependency — cash on hand, burn, runway, planned use of funds, next-round trigger, and debt/project-finance obligations. Historical round-by-round chronology lives in Company Overview; refer to that chronology in prose, but do not copy Company Overview claim ids; if Financials needs a funding fact, mint a local Financials claim with its own sourceRefs.
Financial verdict on revenue quality, margin path, capital intensity, and diligence blockers.

plannedTables.name: Revenue streams table
plannedTables.requirement: stream, mechanism, unit, current value/status, quality, diligence ask.

plannedTables.name: Pricing / monetization table
plannedTables.requirement: price/unit/contract, list vs realized pricing, discounts/unknowns, source.

plannedTables.name: Unit economics table
plannedTables.requirement: metric, value/null, confidence, why it matters, diligence ask.

plannedTables.name: Capital adequacy table
plannedTables.requirement: cash on hand, monthly burn, runway months, planned use of funds, next-round trigger, debt/project-finance obligations; refer to the Company Overview funding chronology without copying its claim ids, and mint local Financials claims only for financing facts needed in this table.

plannedTables.name: Public financial gaps table
plannedTables.requirement: missing private metrics, impact, exact diligence path.

plannedFigures.name: Revenue model bridge
plannedFigures.requirement: How customer activity converts into revenue and gross profit.

plannedFigures.name: Unit economics bridge
plannedFigures.requirement: Use inputs when available; otherwise qualitative nodes with approximationNotes.

plannedFigures.name: Financial estimate range
plannedFigures.requirement: Revenue, burn, runway, margin, or valuation-input ranges with source-backed bounds.

plannedFigures.name: Capital intensity / cash-flow map
plannedFigures.requirement: Capex, inventory, project finance, manufacturing scale-up, or credit exposure.

evidenceStrategy: Revenue/pricing from official pricing, customer contracts/examples, filings, investor announcements, channel data.

Capital adequacy from latest reported cash position, burn signals, hiring/layoffs, debt/credit facilities, and credible investor coverage. Use Company Overview as background for the funding chronology, but do not copy its claim ids across chapters.
Unit economics from public-company comps, customer case studies, cost benchmarks, partner terms, channel fees.
Adverse checks for burn, margin compression, pricing pressure, defaults, inventory issues, customer concentration.
qualityBar: Separate list pricing, company-claimed traction, corroborated metrics, estimates, and unavailable private data.
Do not force SaaS, marketplace, or hardware metrics unless the revenue mechanism supports them.
Do not restate the historical funding chronology; reference it from Company Overview and focus on forward capital adequacy.
Official pricing is list pricing, not realized revenue or margin.
Every null unit-economics field needs a specific diligence request.
Scenario inputs must be labeled public, estimated, or unavailable.
